sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a complaint of shortness of breath and feeling like he catches his breath a lot more than he used to, despite considering himself to be pretty healthy.
He reports that the symptoms started suddenly and without any apparent reason, and that he gets shortness of breath mid-exercise.
He also mentions that his eyes water, but attributes it to allergies, and that his dog also suffers from eye issues.
He has a history of heavy drinking and reports frequent diarrhea after heavy drinking.
He denies any nausea, vomiting, rash, lip or throat swelling, and allergies.
He reports that his symptoms occur in all three situations: at home when the cat is not around, outside, and near his cat.
Past medical history: The patient moved to a new city about two years ago and bought a plot of land where he hunts deer.
He has a full refrigerator at home.
He mentions that he gets eyebugers like his dog.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: The patient uses an albuterol inhaler as prescribed by the urgent care for shortness of breath.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's pulse is okay, pulse ox is normal, and temperature is not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient does not appear in any distress, no rash is appreciated, no angioedema, and no audible stridor.
There is faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields.
Test Results: The patient had a chest x-ray and pulmonary function test, both of which were normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has newly diagnosed allergic asthma.
Problem list: Allergic asthma; confirmed; Asthma status: confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed albuterol inhaler to be used as needed for shortness of breath.
I also prescribed Singulair 10mg to be taken once daily to decrease the occurrences of asthma symptoms.
I will proceed with allergy testing, starting with skin testing, to target the triggers of the patient's asthma.
I will see the patient next week to schedule the skin testing.
Follow-up: I will see the patient next week to schedule the skin testing.
